meta.title: Late seizures after meningioma surgery (Zhang nomogram)
meta.authors: Zhang P, Li Y, Zhang J, et al.
meta.outcome: Probability of late postoperative seizures (>7 days after surgery) after meningioma resection, from 5 factors
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. A nomogram (Zhang et al., J Clin Neurosci 2020) estimating the probability of late postoperative seizures (occurring >7 days after surgery) after meningioma resection, from five clinical/tumour factors. Single-centre, 318 patients (late-seizure rate 17.6%; nomogram C-index 0.79).

How to read it

  • Each factor present raises the probability. Strongest: tumour recurrence/progression and medical/surgical complications; then size ≥3.5 cm, preoperative seizures, and cerebral convexity location.
  • A patient with no factors has ~7% risk; with all five, >90%.

Single-centre model without external validation — a demonstration/counselling aid.

predictors.name: Preoperative seizures
predictors.assess: Any seizure before surgery. Present raises late-seizure risk (OR 2.31).

predictors.name: Cerebral convexity location
predictors.assess: Tumour at the cerebral convexity (vs skull base / parasagittal / posterior fossa). OR 2.17.

predictors.name: Medical / surgical complications
predictors.assess: Peri-operative complications (haemorrhage, hydrocephalus, CNS/wound infection, pneumonia, DVT). OR 4.00.

predictors.name: Tumour recurrence / progression
predictors.assess: Tumour recurrence or progression during follow-up. OR 4.32 (strongest factor).

model.note: Late seizures = >7 days after surgery. Single-centre model (n=318); intercept calibrated to the paper's nomogram worked example (213 points → 63%).
model.viz_info: Each factor present adds its published log-odds (β) to a logistic model; the sum plus a constant gives the probability of late postoperative seizures. The waterfall shows each factor's contribution.

provenance.source: Zhang 2020 J Clin Neurosci Table 2 (β) + Fig 3 nomogram worked example (213 pts→0.632) — doi 10.1016/j.jocn.2020.06.005, PMID 32651139
provenance.notes: Logistic: 5 binary β (preop-sz 0.837, convexity 0.776, size≥3.5 0.943, med/surg-compl 1.385, recurrence 1.463); intercept -2.641 recovered from worked example (Σβ 3.182→p 0.632). Verified: all-absent 6.6% (nomogram floor ~0.05), all-present 94.1% (nomogram max 0.94), worked example 63.2%. WHO grade & neuro-complications entered but not retained. C-index 0.785. Built 2026-07-08.
